key_observations: Severe volume collapse: 73% drop from Week 1 (23.6km) to Week 5 (6.3km) with only 1 run per week since May 13—indicates injury recovery, detraining, or life disruption
Pace stability maintained (5:39–6:18 min/km) despite reduced volume suggests aerobic base preservation, but insufficient stimulus for fitness development
12-day gap (May 13→May 29) and extended inactivity periods (May 19-28, June 11-21) disrupt training continuity and aerobic adaptations
warnings: Acute detraining risk: 8 weeks of sub-threshold volume will erode VO2max and race-specific fitness if this pattern continues
Inconsistent commitment: average 1.5 runs/week with irregular scheduling suggests external constraints (injury, illness, motivation) requiring direct assessment
No heart rate data available: inability to monitor intensity, recovery, or aerobic drift limits coaching precision
recommendations: Conduct injury/health screening: determine if tapering is intentional (pre-race) or forced (pain/illness). If forced, return to 3–4 runs/week at 70% aerobic intensity once cleared
Rebuild structured consistency: establish fixed run days (e.g., Mon/Wed/Fri + long run Sunday) with 20–25km weekly minimum to halt fitness decay
Enable HR monitoring: sync chest strap or enable wrist HR on device to quantify intensity zones and prevent drift toward all easy/all hard pacing

weeks_analysis.assessment: Strong baseline: 3 runs, solid aerobic volume, consistent 6+ min/km pace suggests fitness foundation intact
First warning sign: 42% drop to single run. Possible injury onset or external disruption—pace still solid (6:14/km on long run)
Continued decline: 12-day gap before single 9.5km run. Detraining acceleration phase; frequency loss more concerning than volume
Critical threshold crossed: single 7.2km run represents 70% loss from Week 1. Minimal stimulus for aerobic adaptation
Nadir: lowest volume (6.3km), but pace still 5:47/km—suggests ability to run but unwillingness/inability to commit consistent frequency
Slight recovery attempt: 11-day gap, then 2 runs totaling 9.9km. Inconsistent but positive trend reversal if sustained
